---
title: "UI_P007: Search Input Optimization Principle"
subtitle: "Choose search input components based on dataset size, performance requirements, and UI consistency needs, implementing server-side filtering for large datasets and optimizing user experience"
chapter: "CH04"
category: "principle"
number: "UI_P007"
date-created: "2025-04-07"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "UI Components Law"
article: 
  number: 7
  title: "Search Input Optimization Principle"
related_to:
  - "UI_P006: UI Package Consistency Principle"
  - "DEV_P012: Reactive Data Filtering Principle"
  - "UI_P003: Sidebar Filtering Only"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 7: Search Input Optimization Principle {#overview}

**UI Components Law Article 7 (Search Input Optimization Principle)**

Choose search input components based on dataset size, performance requirements, and UI consistency needs, implementing server-side filtering for large datasets and optimizing user experience with appropriate element types.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Intelligently select search components based on data volume and performance requirements, implementing appropriate filtering strategies to ensure optimal user experience.
:::

# Section 1: Decision Framework {#decision-framework}

## Subsection 1: Dataset Size Analysis {#dataset-size-analysis}

Select input components based on data volume:

| Dataset Size | Recommended Approach | Implementation Strategy |
|-------------|----------------------|-------------------------|
| <1,000 items | Client-side filtering with standard components | selectInput + local search |
| 1,000-10,000 items | Server-side filtering with debouncing | selectizeInput + server processing |
| >10,000 items | Database-level filtering | SQL queries + pagination |

### Performance Criteria Standards {#performance-criteria}

Consider these performance metrics when selecting components:

1. **Initial Load Time**: Time for component first render
2. **Search Response Time**: Delay from user input to result display
3. **Memory Usage**: Memory footprint of component and its data
4. **Network Transfer**: Data transfer between client and server
5. **User Experience Fluidity**: Overall interaction smoothness

## Subsection 2: Component Options Comparison {#component-comparison}

### selectizeInput Features {#selectize-input}

**Pros:**
- Built into Shiny core (no additional dependencies)
- Server-side filtering support via updateSelectizeInput
- Tight integration with Shiny reactive system
- Well-documented and widely used
- Efficient for large datasets with server-side processing

**Cons:**
- Less modern appearance
- Limited visual styling customization options
- Limited mobile experience optimization

**Best For:**
- Applications focused on performance with large datasets
- Situations requiring tight integration with Shiny reactivity
- When minimizing dependencies is important

```r
# Server-side processing implementation example
selectizeInput(
  "customer_search",
  "Search Customer:",
  choices = NULL,
  selectize = TRUE,
  options = list(
    placeholder = "Type name or email...",
    maxproducts = 1,
    maxOptions = 10,
    searchConjunction = "and",  # Support multi-keyword search
    plugins = list("remove_button")  # Remove button
  )
)

# Server-side handler
observeEvent(input$customer_search_search, {
  search_term <- input$customer_search_search
  
  if (nchar(search_term) >= 2) {
    # Database query filtering
    filtered_results <- tbl2(conn, "customers") %>%
      filter(
        str_detect(tolower(name), tolower(!!search_term)) |
        str_detect(tolower(email), tolower(!!search_term))
      ) %>%
      arrange(name) %>%
      head(10) %>%
      collect()
    
    # Update choices
    choices_list <- setNames(
      filtered_results$id,
      paste(filtered_results$name, "(", filtered_results$email, ")")
    )
    
    updateSelectizeInput(
      session,
      "customer_search",
      choices = choices_list
    )
  }
})
```

### shinyWidgets::pickerInput Features {#picker-input}

**Pros:**
- Modern appearance with Bootstrap styling
- Rich customization options
- Better mobile support
- More configurable searching behavior
- Visual consistency with other shinyWidgets components

**Cons:**
- Requires additional package dependency
- Less efficient with extremely large datasets
- More complex to configure server-side processing
- Potentially slower for very large option sets

**Best For:**
- Applications prioritizing modern UI appearance
- Interfaces requiring extensive styling customization
- Applications with mobile usage requirements
- When UI consistency is prioritized over raw performance

```r
# Implementation example
shinyWidgets::pickerInput(
  "customer_search",
  "Search Customer:",
  choices = filtered_data,
  options = list(
    "live-search" = TRUE,
    "live-search-placeholder" = "Type name or email...",
    "size" = 5,
    "liveSearchStyle" = "contains",
    "maxOptions" = 10,
    "maxOptionsText" = "Showing first {n} results",
    "noneResultsText" = "No matching results",
    "style" = "btn-outline-primary"
  ),
  multiple = FALSE
)
```

### DT::datatable Search Functionality {#datatable-search}

**Applicable Scenarios:**
- When displaying detailed data tables
- Supporting multi-column simultaneous search
- Requiring sorting and pagination functionality

```r
# Advanced table search
DT::datatable(
  customers_data,
  filter = "top",
  options = list(
    searchHighlight = TRUE,
    search = list(regex = TRUE, caseInsensitive = TRUE),
    columnDefs = list(
      list(searchable = FALSE, targets = c(0, 1))  # Some columns not searchable
    )
  )
)
```

# Section 2: Implementation Guidelines {#implementation-guidelines}

## Subsection 1: Server-Side Processing {#server-side-processing}

For datasets with more than 1,000 items, implement server-side filtering:

### selectizeInput Server-Side Implementation {#selectize-server}

```r
# Complete server-side search implementation
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Debounced search
  search_reactive <- reactive({
    input$customer_search_search
  }) %>% debounce(300)  # 300ms delay
  
  observeEvent(search_reactive(), {
    search_term <- search_reactive()
    
    if (is.null(search_term) || nchar(search_term) < 2) {
      updateSelectizeInput(
        session,
        "customer_search",
        choices = list(),
        server = TRUE
      )
      return()
    }
    
    # Execute search query
    tryCatch({
      results <- search_customers(search_term)
      
      choices_list <- setNames(
        results$id,
        paste0(results$name, " <", results$email, ">")
      )
      
      updateSelectizeInput(
        session,
        "customer_search",
        choices = choices_list,
        server = TRUE
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      showNotification(
        paste("Search error:", e$message),
        type = "error"
      )
    })
  })
}

# Search function
search_customers <- function(term, limit = 10) {
  tbl2(conn, "customers") %>%
    filter(
      str_detect(tolower(name), tolower(!!term)) |
      str_detect(tolower(email), tolower(!!term)) |
      str_detect(tolower(company), tolower(!!term))
    ) %>%
    arrange(name) %>%
    head(limit) %>%
    collect()
}
```

### pickerInput Optimization Implementation {#picker-optimization}

```r
# Performance-optimized version of pickerInput
optimized_picker_search <- function(data_source, search_columns) {
  # Pre-process data to improve search performance
  processed_data <- data_source %>%
    mutate(
      search_text = tolower(paste(
        across(all_of(search_columns)), 
        sep = " "
      ))
    )
  
  # Client-side search (suitable for medium-sized datasets)
  shinyWidgets::updatePickerInput(
    session,
    "search_input",
    choices = processed_data,
    options = list(
      "liveSearchNormalize" = TRUE,
      "liveSearchStyle" = "startsWith"
    )
  )
}
```

## Subsection 2: Search Optimization Techniques {#search-optimization}

### Minimum Character Threshold {#minimum-character-threshold}

```r
# Implement minimum character requirement
observeEvent(input$search_input_search, {
  search_term <- input$search_input_search
  
  if (nchar(search_term) < 2) {
    # Clear results or show hint
    updateSelectizeInput(
      session,
      "search_input",
      choices = list("Please enter at least 2 characters" = "")
    )
    return()
  }
  
  # Execute search
  perform_search(search_term)
})
```

### Debouncing {#debouncing}

```r
# Debounced search implementation
search_debounced <- reactive({
  input$search_input_search
}) %>% debounce(500)  # 500ms delay

observeEvent(search_debounced(), {
  if (nchar(search_debounced()) >= 2) {
    perform_search(search_debounced())
  }
})
```

### Result Limiting {#result-limiting}

```r
# Limit search result count
search_with_limit <- function(term, max_results = 15) {
  all_results <- perform_database_search(term)
  
  if (nrow(all_results) > max_results) {
    limited_results <- all_results[1:max_results, ]
    
    # Add "more results" indicator
    additional_info <- data.frame(
      id = "more_results",
      display_text = paste("...", nrow(all_results) - max_results, "more results")
    )
    
    return(rbind(limited_results, additional_info))
  }
  
  return(all_results)
}
```

### Search Vectorization {#search-vectorization}

```r
# Use vectorized operations for improved search performance
vectorized_search <- function(data, search_terms, search_columns) {
  # Build search pattern
  pattern <- paste(search_terms, collapse = "|")
  
  # Vectorized search
  matches <- data %>%
    filter(
      if_any(
        all_of(search_columns),
        ~ str_detect(tolower(.x), tolower(pattern))
      )
    )
  
  return(matches)
}

# Use grep for efficient search
grep_search <- function(data, term, column) {
  matches <- grep(
    pattern = tolower(term),
    x = tolower(data[[column]]),
    value = FALSE,
    fixed = TRUE  # Use fixed string matching for better performance
  )
  
  return(data[matches, ])
}
```

### Incremental Loading {#incremental-loading}

```r
# Paginated incremental loading
incremental_search <- function(term, page = 1, page_size = 20) {
  offset <- (page - 1) * page_size
  
  results <- tbl2(conn, "large_dataset") %>%
    filter(str_detect(tolower(name), tolower(!!term))) %>%
    arrange(name) %>%
    slice(offset + 1:(offset + page_size)) %>%
    collect()
  
  return(list(
    data = results,
    has_more = nrow(results) == page_size
  ))
}
```

## Subsection 3: User Experience Considerations {#ux-considerations}

### Meaningful Labels {#meaningful-labels}

```r
# Create descriptive labels
create_search_labels <- function(data) {
  labels <- paste0(
    data$name,
    " (", data$email, ")",
    if_else(
      !is.na(data$company),
      paste(" - ", data$company),
      ""
    )
  )
  
  return(setNames(data$id, labels))
}

# Use rich text labels
rich_labels <- function(data) {
  labels <- paste0(
    "<strong>", data$name, "</strong><br>",
    "<small class='text-muted'>", data$email, "</small>"
  )
  
  return(setNames(data$id, labels))
}
```

### Status Feedback {#status-feedback}

```r
# Loading indicator
show_search_loading <- function() {
  showNotification(
    tagList(
      icon("spinner", class = "fa-spin"),
      "Searching..."
    ),
    id = "search_loading",
    duration = NULL,
    type = "message"
  )
}

hide_search_loading <- function() {
  removeNotification("search_loading")
}

# Search result statistics
show_search_stats <- function(count, term) {
  output$search_stats <- renderText({
    if (count == 0) {
      paste("No results found for '", term, "'")
    } else {
      paste("Found", count, "results for '", term, "'")
    }
  })
}
```

### Empty State Handling {#empty-state-handling}

```r
# Empty result handling
handle_empty_results <- function(search_term) {
  updateSelectizeInput(
    session,
    "search_input",
    choices = list(
      paste("No results found for '", search_term, "'") = ""
    )
  )
  
  # Suggest alternative searches
  suggestions <- get_search_suggestions(search_term)
  if (length(suggestions) > 0) {
    showNotification(
      tagList(
        p("Did you mean:"),
        tags$ul(
          lapply(suggestions, function(s) {
            tags$li(
              actionLink(
                paste0("suggestion_", s),
                s,
                onclick = paste0("Shiny.setInputValue('search_suggestion', '", s, "')") 
              )
            )
          })
        )
      ),
      type = "warning",
      duration = 10
    )
  }
}

# Search suggestion generation
get_search_suggestions <- function(term) {
  # Suggestions based on edit distance
  all_names <- get_all_searchable_names()
  distances <- adist(tolower(term), tolower(all_names))
  close_matches <- all_names[distances <= 2]
  
  return(head(close_matches, 3))
}
```

# Section 3: Application Contexts {#application-contexts}

## Subsection 1: Customer Search {#customer-search}

Customer search functionality follows these guidelines:

### Small Customer Base {#small-customer-base}

```r
# <500 customers: Use standard selectizeInput with client-side filtering
ui_small_customers <- fluidPage(
  selectizeInput(
    "customer_select",
    "Select Customer:",
    choices = create_customer_choices(customers_data),
    options = list(
      placeholder = "Type customer name...",
      searchField = c("text", "value")
    )
  )
)

create_customer_choices <- function(data) {
  choices <- setNames(
    data$customer_id,
    paste0(data$name, " - ", data$email)
  )
  return(choices)
}
```

### Medium Customer Base {#medium-customer-base}

```r
# 500-5,000 customers: Use selectizeInput with server=TRUE
ui_medium_customers <- fluidPage(
  selectizeInput(
    "customer_select",
    "Search Customer:",
    choices = NULL,
    options = list(
      placeholder = "Type name, email, or company...",
      maxproducts = 1,
      maxOptions = 15
    )
  )
)

server_medium_customers <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Server-side search
  updateSelectizeInput(
    session,
    "customer_select",
    choices = customer_choices_reactive(),
    server = TRUE
  )
  
  customer_choices_reactive <- reactive({
    req(input$customer_select_search)
    search_customers_server(
      term = input$customer_select_search,
      limit = 15
    )
  })
}
```

### Large Customer Base {#large-customer-base}

```r
# >5,000 customers: Implement database-level filtering
ui_large_customers <- fluidPage(
  div(
    class = "search-container",
    selectizeInput(
      "customer_search",
      "Search Customer:",
      choices = NULL,
      options = list(
        placeholder = "Please enter at least 3 characters to search...",
        maxproducts = 1,
        maxOptions = 20,
        loadThrottle = 300
      )
    ),
    div(
      id = "search_status",
      class = "search-status"
    )
  )
)

server_large_customers <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Database-level search with caching
  search_cache <- reactiveVal(list())
  
  observeEvent(input$customer_search_search, {
    search_term <- input$customer_search_search
    
    if (nchar(search_term) < 3) {
      output$search_status <- renderText("Please enter at least 3 characters")
      return()
    }
    
    # Check cache
    cache_key <- tolower(search_term)
    cached_result <- search_cache()[[cache_key]]
    
    if (!is.null(cached_result)) {
      update_search_results(cached_result)
      return()
    }
    
    # Execute database search
    show_search_loading()
    
    future({
      search_customers_db(search_term)
    }) %...>% {
      # Update cache
      current_cache <- search_cache()
      current_cache[[cache_key]] <- .
      search_cache(current_cache)
      
      # Update results
      update_search_results(.)
      hide_search_loading()
    }
  })
}

search_customers_db <- function(term, limit = 20) {
  sql <- "
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      name,
      email,
      company
    FROM customers 
    WHERE 
      LOWER(name) LIKE LOWER($1) OR
      LOWER(email) LIKE LOWER($1) OR
      LOWER(company) LIKE LOWER($1)
    ORDER BY 
      CASE WHEN LOWER(name) LIKE LOWER($1) THEN 1 ELSE 2 END,
      name
    LIMIT $2
  "
  
  results <- dbGetQuery(
    conn, 
    sql, 
    params = list(
      paste0("%", term, "%"),
      limit
    )
  )
  
  return(results)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Product Search {#product-search}

Product catalogs often require specialized handling:

### Hierarchical Category Search {#hierarchical-category-search}

```r
# Consider category hierarchies: Allow filtering by category before searching
ui_product_search <- fluidPage(
  fluidRow(
    column(3,
      selectInput(
        "category_filter",
        "Product Category:",
        choices = c("All" = "all", get_product_categories())
      )
    ),
    column(9,
      selectizeInput(
        "product_search",
        "Search Products:",
        choices = NULL,
        options = list(
          placeholder = "Type product name or keywords...",
          maxOptions = 20
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server_product_search <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Category-based product search
  observeEvent(c(input$product_search_search, input$category_filter), {
    search_term <- input$product_search_search
    category <- input$category_filter
    
    if (nchar(search_term) >= 2) {
      results <- search_products(
        term = search_term,
        category = if (category == "all") NULL else category
      )
      
      choices_list <- create_product_choices(results)
      
      updateSelectizeInput(
        session,
        "product_search",
        choices = choices_list
      )
    }
  })
}

search_products <- function(term, category = NULL, limit = 20) {
  query <- tbl2(conn, "products") %>%
    filter(
      str_detect(tolower(name), tolower(!!term)) |
      str_detect(tolower(description), tolower(!!term)) |
      str_detect(tolower(sku), tolower(!!term))
    )
  
  if (!is.null(category)) {
    query <- query %>% filter(category == !!category)
  }
  
  results <- query %>%
    arrange(name) %>%
    head(limit) %>%
    collect()
  
  return(results)
}

create_product_choices <- function(products) {
  choices <- setNames(
    products$product_id,
    paste0(
      products$name,
      " - $", products$price,
      if_else(
        products$in_stock,
        " (In Stock)",
        " (Out of Stock)"
      )
    )
  )
  return(choices)
}
```

### Faceted Search {#faceted-search}

```r
# Implement faceted search: Enable multiple filtering dimensions
ui_faceted_search <- fluidPage(
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      h4("Filter Options"),
      selectInput(
        "brand_filter",
        "Brand:",
        choices = c("All" = "all", get_brands()),
        multiple = TRUE
      ),
      sliderInput(
        "price_range",
        "Price Range:",
        min = 0,
        max = 1000,
        value = c(0, 1000)
      ),
      checkboxGroupInput(
        "features",
        "Product Features:",
        choices = get_product_features()
      )
    ),
    mainPanel(
      selectizeInput(
        "product_search",
        "Search Products:",
        choices = NULL,
        width = "100%"
      ),
      div(id = "search_results")
    )
  )
)

server_faceted_search <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Combine multiple filter conditions
  filtered_products <- reactive({
    base_query <- tbl2(conn, "products")
    
    # Brand filter
    if (!is.null(input$brand_filter) && !"all" %in% input$brand_filter) {
      base_query <- base_query %>%
        filter(brand %in% !!input$brand_filter)
    }
    
    # Price filter
    base_query <- base_query %>%
      filter(
        price >= !!input$price_range[1],
        price <= !!input$price_range[2]
      )
    
    # Features filter
    if (length(input$features) > 0) {
      feature_conditions <- map_chr(input$features, ~paste0("features LIKE '%", .x, "%'"))
      feature_sql <- paste(feature_conditions, collapse = " OR ")
      base_query <- base_query %>%
        filter(sql(paste("(", feature_sql, ")")))
    }
    
    return(base_query)
  })
  
  # Search based on filtered results
  observeEvent(input$product_search_search, {
    search_term <- input$product_search_search
    
    if (nchar(search_term) >= 2) {
      results <- filtered_products() %>%
        filter(
          str_detect(tolower(name), tolower(!!search_term)) |
          str_detect(tolower(description), tolower(!!search_term))
        ) %>%
        head(25) %>%
        collect()
      
      choices_list <- create_detailed_product_choices(results)
      
      updateSelectizeInput(
        session,
        "product_search",
        choices = choices_list
      )
    }
  })
}
```

### Include Product Metadata {#product-metadata}

```r
# Display prices, availability, etc. in results
create_detailed_product_choices <- function(products) {
  choices <- setNames(
    products$product_id,
    paste0(
      "<div class='product-choice'>",
      "<strong>", products$name, "</strong>",
      "<br><span class='price'>$", products$price, "</span>",
      "<span class='stock ", 
      if_else(products$in_stock, "in-stock", "out-stock"),
      "'>", 
      if_else(products$in_stock, "In Stock", "Out of Stock"),
      "</span>",
      "<br><small class='description'>",
      str_trunc(products$description, 50),
      "</small>",
      "</div>"
    )
  )
  return(choices)
}

# Corresponding CSS styles
product_search_css <- tags$style("
  .product-choice {
    padding: 8px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #eee;
  }
  .product-choice:last-child {
    border-bottom: none;
  }
  .price {
    color: #28a745;
    font-weight: bold;
    margin-right: 10px;
  }
  .stock.in-stock {
    color: #28a745;
  }
  .stock.out-stock {
    color: #dc3545;
  }
  .description {
    color: #6c757d;
  }
")
```

# Section 4: Performance Monitoring and Testing {#performance-testing}

## Subsection 1: Performance Benchmarking {#performance-benchmarking}

```r
# Search performance benchmarking
benchmark_search_performance <- function() {
  test_sizes <- c(100, 1000, 10000, 50000)
  search_terms <- c("A", "An", "John", "Smith")
  
  results <- expand_grid(
    size = test_sizes,
    term = search_terms
  ) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      timing = list({
        test_data <- generate_test_customers(size)
        
        # Test different search methods
        times <- microbenchmark(
          client_side = grep(tolower(term), tolower(test_data$name)),
          vectorized = str_detect(tolower(test_data$name), tolower(term)),
          regex = grepl(tolower(term), tolower(test_data$name), fixed = TRUE),
          times = 10
        )
        
        summary(times)
      })
    )
  
  return(results)
}

# Memory usage testing
test_memory_usage <- function(data_size) {
  # Record memory before test
  mem_before <- pryr::mem_used()
  
  # Execute search operation
  test_data <- generate_test_customers(data_size)
  search_results <- perform_search(test_data, "test")
  
  # Record memory after test
  mem_after <- pryr::mem_used()
  
  return(list(
    memory_used = mem_after - mem_before,
    data_size = data_size,
    result_count = nrow(search_results)
  ))
}
```

## Subsection 2: Real-Time Performance Monitoring {#real-time-monitoring}

```r
# Real-time search performance monitoring
monitor_search_performance <- function() {
  # Performance monitoring reactive value
  search_metrics <- reactiveVal(data.frame(
    timestamp = as.POSIXct(character()),
    search_term = character(),
    result_count = integer(),
    processing_time = numeric(),
    memory_usage = numeric()
  ))
  
  # Wrap search function to add monitoring
  monitored_search <- function(term) {
    start_time <- Sys.time()
    start_memory <- pryr::mem_used()
    
    # Execute search
    results <- perform_search(term)
    
    end_time <- Sys.time()
    end_memory <- pryr::mem_used()
    
    # Record metrics
    new_metric <- data.frame(
      timestamp = Sys.time(),
      search_term = term,
      result_count = nrow(results),
      processing_time = as.numeric(end_time - start_time),
      memory_usage = as.numeric(end_memory - start_memory)
    )
    
    # Update metrics
    current_metrics <- search_metrics()
    updated_metrics <- rbind(current_metrics, new_metric)
    
    # Keep last 100 records
    if (nrow(updated_metrics) > 100) {
      updated_metrics <- tail(updated_metrics, 100)
    }
    
    search_metrics(updated_metrics)
    
    # Performance warning
    if (new_metric$processing_time > 2) {
      showNotification(
        paste("Search processing time is high:", round(new_metric$processing_time, 2), "seconds"),
        type = "warning"
      )
    }
    
    return(results)
  }
  
  return(list(
    search_function = monitored_search,
    metrics = search_metrics
  ))
}

# Performance dashboard
create_performance_dashboard <- function(metrics_reactive) {
  output$performance_plot <- renderPlot({
    metrics <- metrics_reactive()
    
    if (nrow(metrics) > 0) {
      ggplot(metrics, aes(x = timestamp, y = processing_time)) +
        geom_line(color = "blue") +
        geom_point(aes(size = result_count), alpha = 0.6) +
        labs(
          title = "Search Performance Monitoring",
          x = "Time",
          y = "Processing Time (seconds)",
          size = "Result Count"
        ) +
        theme_minimal()
    }
  })
  
  output$performance_summary <- renderTable({
    metrics <- metrics_reactive()
    
    if (nrow(metrics) > 0) {
      metrics %>%
        summarise(
          "Average Processing Time" = round(mean(processing_time), 3),
          "Max Processing Time" = round(max(processing_time), 3),
          "Average Result Count" = round(mean(result_count)),
          "Search Count" = n()
        )
    }
  })
}
```

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Search Input Optimization Principle ensures that applications provide responsive, user-friendly search functionality across various scales by intelligently selecting appropriate search components and implementing strategies based on dataset size, performance requirements, and user experience goals. Through server-side filtering, debouncing, result limiting, and intelligent caching techniques, applications can maintain excellent performance while providing consistent and intuitive search experiences.

---

*Based on: UI Components Law Article 7*  
*Related to: [UI_P006 UI Package Consistency Principle](UI_P006_ui_package_consistency.qmd), [DEV_P012 Reactive Data Filtering Principle](../../CH03_development_methodology/principles/DEV_P012_reactive_data_filtering.qmd)*
